slides.title: Google Home & Smart Home Ecosystem
slides.subtitle: Comprehensive Guide to Nest Hardware, Matter, Thread, Developer APIs & Gemini AI

slides.title: Evolution of Google Smart Home
slides.subtitle: From a simple smart speaker to a unified ambient computing platform

list.text: 2014: Nest Acquisition - Google acquires Nest Labs for $3.2B, marking its entry into connected home hardware.
2016: Google Home Launch - Introduction of the original Google Home speaker powered by Google Assistant.
2019: Google Nest Rebrand - Rebranding of home products to 'Google Nest', merging Nest and Google Home teams.
2022: Matter 1.0 Release - Google announces major support for Matter standard, updating existing Nest devices.
2024: Gemini AI Integration - Infusing Gemini LLM capabilities into Google Assistant for complex routine generation and natural conversation.

timeline.impact: Introduced Google Assistant in the home

timeline.event: Nest devices updated with Matter support
Gemini models roll out to Nest displays

list.text: Invisibility: Technology fades into the background, responding to presence, routines, and gestures rather than active screen time.
Natural Interfaces: Interfaces shift from screens to voice, gaze, gestures, and automated triggers.
Context Awareness: The home senses who is in a room, the time of day, and history, making proactive optimizations.

slides.title: Mobile-First vs Ambient UI
leftPoints: Requires picking up a phone, unlocking it, and opening a dedicated application.
Requires precise taps and manual configurations for basic actions.
Reactive model: the user must request all state changes manually.
rightPoints: Responds instantly to voice, presence sensors, or touchscreens mounted on walls.
Understands natural context (e.g. 'Good morning' triggers multiple actions).
Proactive model: uses environmental inputs (motion, light, humidity) to act automatically.

slides.title: The Nest Hardware Ecosystem
slides.subtitle: Overview of physical devices powering the Google Home experience

slides.title: Smart Speakers vs Smart Displays
leftPoints: Acoustic focus: Optimized for high-fidelity audio (Nest Audio) or compact placement (Nest Mini).
Interaction: Purely voice-controlled via Google Assistant with top touch controls.
Use Cases: Background music, quick voice commands, and smart home notifications.
slides.rightTitle: Smart Displays (Nest Hub & Nest Hub Max)
rightPoints: Visual feedback: Screen displays weather, recipes, calendar events, and video feeds from Nest Cams.
Gestures & Camera: Hub Max includes a camera for Nest Cam monitoring, Face Match, and hand gestures.
Use Cases: Kitchen assistant, smart home control center, photo frame, and video calling.

slides.title: Nest Mini & Nest Audio Deep Dive

list.text: Nest Audio: Features a 75mm woofer and 19mm tweeter, designed to deliver rich bass and clear vocals. Adapts dynamically to room acoustics.
Nest Mini (2nd Gen): Features a custom 40mm driver, wall-mount slot, and an on-device machine learning chip with 1 TeraOPS compute for faster local voice responses.
Far-field Microphones: Uses multi-microphone arrays and echo cancellation technology to detect 'Hey Google' commands even during loud music playback.

slides.title: Nest Hub & Nest Hub Max Deep Dive

list.text: Nest Hub (2nd Gen): 7-inch display featuring Soli radar technology for Sleep Sensing (breathing & movement detection) and touchless gesture control.
Nest Hub Max: 10-inch HD screen with a 6.5MP wide-angle camera. Supports Face Match, video calling, and doubles as an indoor Nest Cam.
Ambient EQ: Dynamically adjusts screen brightness and color temperature to match the lighting of the room, looking like a real photo frame.
Nest Learning Thermostat: Uses sensors and algorithms to learn temperature preferences over time and automatically creates a schedule.
Eco Temperature: Uses built-in motion sensors and phone location (geofencing) to detect when the house is empty, switching to Eco mode to save energy.
System Compatibility: Operates with low-voltage heating and cooling systems, and supports Matter for cross-ecosystem automation.
Nest Cam (Indoor/Outdoor): Offers 1080p HDR video with night vision. Integrates on-device Edge AI to differentiate between people, animals, packages, and vehicles.
Nest Doorbell (Wired/Battery): Features a tall 3:4 aspect ratio to view packages on the floor, and supports pre-recorded quick responses.
Local Backup: Includes on-device memory storage that records up to 1 hour of video locally during Wi-Fi outages.
Split-Spectrum Sensor: Uses two wavelengths of light to detect both fast-burning and slow-smoldering fires quickly.
Voice Alerts: Instead of a loud siren, it speaks clearly (e.g. 'Heads up. There's smoke in the kitchen') before triggering full alarm.
App Integration: Sends smartphone alerts and tells you exactly what the issue is, allowing you to silence the alarm from your phone.

slides.title: Smart Home Mesh: Nest Wifi Pro

list.text: Wi-Fi 6E Speed: Operates on the newly opened 6GHz wireless band, providing faster speeds, lower latency, and less congestion.
Built-in Thread Border Router: Connects Thread smart home devices directly to your home network without extra hubs.
Smart Home Traffic Prioritization: Automatically optimizes bandwidth for video calls, streaming, and smart home signals.

slides.title: Connectivity Protocols
slides.subtitle: Matter and Thread: The foundation of a reliable, secure, and interoperable smart home

slides.title: The Matter & Thread Protocol Stack
slides.note: Matter defines the common language devices speak, while Thread provides a low-power IP-based mesh network layer.

slides.title: The Role of Wi-Fi in Matter

list.text: High Data Rates: Essential for smart home devices that stream media or require high bandwidth, such as smart displays, cameras, and streaming sticks.
Main Powered: Typically used for devices connected to a continuous power source, as Wi-Fi consumes substantial power.
Direct LAN Access: Communicates over the local network using IPv6 without relying on external cloud gateways.
Low Power Consumption: Optimized for battery-operated devices like door locks, temp sensors, and motion detectors to last for years.
Self-Healing Architecture: If a Thread router node fails, the mesh automatically reroutes traffic to ensure connection continuity.
IP Native: Unlike Zigbee or Z-Wave, Thread is built on IPv6, enabling direct IP-to-IP device communication without translations.
Device Discovery: Matter uses BLE during the initial setup to securely discover new, uncommissioned devices nearby.
Credential Handshake: BLE transfers Wi-Fi or Thread network credentials securely from the phone app to the device.
BLE Disconnection: Once onboarding is complete, the device disconnects BLE and communicates solely over Wi-Fi or Thread.
Matter Controller: The brain of the setup. Initiates commissioning, issues commands, and stores the local cryptographic fabric keys. (e.g. Nest Hub 2nd Gen, Nest Wifi Pro).
Matter Bridge: Translates non-Matter protocols (like Zigbee, Z-Wave, or proprietary RF) into Matter-compatible signals, bringing older legacy devices into the ecosystem.
Fabric: A virtual network of trusted devices sharing a common Root of Trust, enabling direct device interaction.
Translating Physical Layers: Connects the Thread mesh network (802.15.4) to the local Wi-Fi or Ethernet network (802.11 / 802.3).
No Cloud Required: Allows any Matter controller (like a phone or hub) to send commands directly to battery-operated Thread devices locally.
Google Devices: Google Nest Hub Max, Nest Hub (2nd Gen), and Nest Wifi Pro all contain built-in Thread Border Routers.
Ecosystem Freedom: A single Matter device can be controlled simultaneously by Google Home, Apple Home, and Amazon Alexa.
Independent Fabrics: Each smart home platform creates its own separate fabric and security keys on the device.
Zero Interference: Actions and states are synchronized locally on the device; one platform cannot lock out another.
Device Attestation Certificate (DAC): Every Matter device has a unique DAC burned in during manufacturing to prove authenticity.
Node Operational Credentials (NOC): Generated locally during setup to represent the device's identity on the specific home fabric.
AES-128-CCM Encryption: All messages passing through the network are encrypted and authenticated to prevent sniffing and replay attacks.

slides.title: Developing for Google Home
slides.subtitle: Building integrations, device drivers, and custom web applications for the Google Home platform

list.text: Console Portal: Dashboard to register Matter vendor IDs (VIDs), product IDs (PIDs), and create virtual testing environments.
OTA Provider: Manage firmware updates for Matter devices and distribute them securely over the air via the Google infrastructure.
Analytics & Logs: Monitor device connection rates, command latencies, and error codes in real time.

box.title: Connecting via the Smart Home API

list.text: OAuth 2.0 Flow: Authenticates the user and links their Google account to the developer's cloud backend account.
Webhook Fulfilment: Google sends HTTPS POST requests representing user commands to the developer's cloud server.
Request Sync: Proactively notifies Google of device name, trait, or connection state changes via a sync call.

box.title: JSON Request from Google Assistant to Developer Cloud
Requesting Current State of Devices

list.text: Hub Container: The SDK runs a JavaScript application inside a Node.js or WebAssembly container directly on Google Nest Hubs or Chromecast.
mDNS/SSDP Discovery: The Google Nest device searches the local network dynamically to detect compatible devices.
Protocol Channels: Executes local commands directly using HTTP/REST, TCP sockets, UDP, or CoAP instead of sending commands to the cloud.

box.title: JavaScript Discovery Setup
JavaScript Command Execution

list.text: Open-Source Core: Based on the Connected Home IP (CHIP) repository containing standard reference code for the Matter stack.
Supported SDKs: Espressif IDF (ESP32), Nordic Semiconductor Connect SDK (nRF52840), and Raspberry Pi Pico SDK.
Cryptographic Engine: Integrates hardware-backed secure elements to process Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) for Matter handshakes.
action.devices.types.LIGHT: Supports brightness, color, temperature, and flash effects.
action.devices.types.LOCK: Supports lock/unlock state, status detection, and lock jam reporting.
action.devices.types.THERMOSTAT: Supports target temperature setting, heating/cooling mode toggling, and fan control.
action.devices.traits.OnOff: The most basic trait. Enables toggling hardware power states (true/false).
action.devices.traits.Brightness: Defines percentage-based dimming levels (0 to 100).
action.devices.traits.ColorSetting: Handles RGB color values (HEX/decimal) and Kelvin color temperature.

slides.title: Advanced Automations & Routines
slides.subtitle: Unlocking the power of the Google Home Script Editor for custom smart home logic

list.text: Web Console access: Edit scripts from your desktop browser at script.home.google.com.
YAML Programming: Author robust automations using structured code containing triggers, states, conditions, and sequential actions.
Sensor Integration: React to micro-changes like luminance, temperature, contact sensor states, and motion.
metadata: Declares the name and descriptive explanation of the automation.
starters: The events that trigger the script execution. Can have multiple triggers (OR logic).
condition: Logical checks evaluated before running the action. Must resolve to true (AND logic).
actions: The list of sequential actions to perform (device commands, delays, notifications).
Time Starter: Triggered at specific times, days of the week, or offset relative to sunrise/sunset (e.g. 'type: time.schedule').
Device State Starter: Triggered when a device property changes (e.g. motion detected, lock turned, light switched on).
Location Starter: Runs based on geofencing (e.g. when the first user arrives home or the last user leaves).
Time Conditions: Restrict actions to certain times (e.g., 'type: time.between', 'after: 22:00', 'before: 06:00').
State Conditions: Checks if another device state is matching (e.g. only run if TV is ON or garage door is OPEN).
Home Mode Conditions: Filters based on whether the home structure state is set to Home or Away.
Device Commands: Execute specific device functions (e.g., 'type: device.command.OnOff', 'on: true').
Delays: Pause execution sequence (e.g., 'type: time.delay', 'duration: 5m') before starting the next action.
Broadcasts & Alerts: Send push notifications to family phones or broadcast custom voice audio on Nest speakers.

box.title: Locking doors when home structure is empty
box.code:
metadata:
  name: Empty House Lockdown
  description: Locks front door and turns off lights when everyone leaves
automations:
  - starters:
      - type: home.state.HomeAway
        state: homeAway
        is: AWAY
    actions:
      - type: device.command.LockUnlock
        devices:
          - Front Door Lock
        lock: true
      - type: device.command.OnOff
        devices:
          - All Lights Group
        on: false

box.title: Adapting lighting based on luminance
box.code:
metadata:
  name: Ambient Reading Mode
  description: Dim lights and adjust color temperature when ambient light drops
automations:
  - starters:
      - type: device.state.LuminanceReport
        device: Living Room Motion Sensor
        state: currentLuminanceLevel
        below: 50
    condition:
      type: device.state.OnOff
      device: Living Room TV
      state: on
      is: false
    actions:
      - type: device.command.OnOff
        devices:
          - Reading Lamp
        on: true
      - type: device.command.ColorSetting
        devices:
          - Reading Lamp
        colorTemperature: 2700

slides.title: The Future of Smart Homes
slides.subtitle: Ambience, Generative AI, and proactive smart home helpers

box.title: The Next Gen AI Smart Assistant

list.text: Contextual Assistant: Understands complex phrases and intent without requiring specific commands or structures.
State Memory: Remembers conversational histories to make dynamic adjustments, like 'make the lights warmer than yesterday'.
LLM at the Edge: Leverages Gemini Nano on-device models to process smart home metadata locally, increasing privacy.
Semantic Understanding: Saying 'It's too bright in here' or 'Make it cozy' translates automatically to dimming the overhead lights and turning on the fireplace.
Fuzzy Instructions: Evaluates vague queries and asks clarifying questions, e.g. 'Should I turn on the desk lamp or the ceiling light?'.
Multi-Intent: Processes multiple actions in a single breath: 'Turn on the fan, dim the lights, and set a timer for 10 minutes'.
Search Video History: Users can query Nest Cams using natural language, e.g. 'Did the postman deliver any packages today?' or 'Where did I leave my keys?'.
Visual Reasoning: Gemini identifies items and event patterns in security feeds without tags, reporting chronological summaries.
Intelligent Highlights: Extracts and compiles short clips based on user query description automatically.
Text-to-Code: Describe an automation in plain English (e.g. 'Turn off the lights if no motion is detected for an hour, but only at night'), and Gemini writes the YAML script.
Error Correction: Identifies syntax bugs or unavailable device attributes, automatically suggesting corrected YAML structures.
One-Click Deployment: Deploys the generated script directly into the Google Home Script Console after validation.
Pattern Tracking: Tracks household behaviors to identify repetitive manual adjustments (e.g. dimming bedroom lights every night at 10 PM).
Proactive Optimization: Prompts the user via Nest Hub screen: 'I noticed you adjust the heat at 7 AM. Would you like me to automate this?'.
Energy Savings: Automatically coordinates appliance cycles with local power grid solar pricing peaks to lower bills.

slides.title: Connectivity Protocol Quiz
slides.question: Which wireless protocol is used by Matter to form a low-power, self-healing mesh network for battery-operated devices?
slides.explanation: Thread is a low-power, IPv6-based self-healing mesh networking protocol that Matter uses for sensors, smart locks, and other low-power battery devices.

slides.title: Automation Scripting Quiz
slides.question: What is the primary language used to write custom automations in the advanced Google Home Script Editor?
slides.explanation: The Google Home Script Editor uses YAML to construct triggers, conditions, and actions for advanced smart home automations.

slides.title: Local Control API Quiz
slides.question: Which Google Home SDK executes JavaScript code locally on a Nest display/Chromecast to control local network devices directly?
slides.explanation: The Local Home SDK executes JavaScript on a local Google Nest device or Chromecast to identify and control smart devices on the local LAN.

slides.title: Interoperability Quiz
slides.question: Which Matter feature allows a single smart home device (e.g. smart light bulb) to be controlled by multiple platforms like Google Home, Apple Home, and Alexa simultaneously?
slides.explanation: The Multi-Admin feature of Matter allows a device to connect securely to multiple independent fabrics (controllers) at the same time.

slides.title: Thank You!
